mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) below prior 12-month levels signals contraction in residential investment, a leading component of aggregate demand that historically precedes broader consumption and credit tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 15% below its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1534K reflects continued decline from 2005-2006 housing peak
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST breaches below the 15% threshold are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains at a restrictive plateau (no cuts for multiple consecutive months) after a tightening cycle, elevated real rates continue to compress equity valuations and discourage risk-taking, producing negative or muted S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days until easing begins.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS unchanged for 6+ consecutive months after prior tightening cycle
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate held near cycle peak into early 2007 following 2004-2006 tightening
regime_hint: monetary policy plateau at cycle peak
failure_condition: If forward 20-day SPX returns during extended FEDFUNDS plateaus average positive and statistically indistinguishable from baseline, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in housing starts (HOUST) alongside decelerating payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates that housing-sector weakness is transmitting into broader labor demand, amplifying downside risk to consumption and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST year-over-year decline exceeds 20% while PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth falls below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1534K down sharply from 2005 peak while PAYEMS growth at 147190K remains modest
failure_condition: If the joint HOUST/PAYEMS trigger condition produces forward 60-day returns not significantly more negative than the HOUST-only signal (interaction term insignificant at p>0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where unemployment (UNRATE) sits near cyclical lows, further declines in UNRATE signal labor market tightness that historically precedes wage-driven margin compression and increased recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when the expansion is mature.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% near multi-year lows as of early 2007
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: If SPX forward 20-day returns following further UNRATE declines during late-cycle regimes are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect reverses sign outside late-cycle windows, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy supply-side output and corporate profitability momentum, which is priced into equities with a short lag, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.5949 reflecting continued modest expansion
regime_hint: any regime with positive industrial output growth
failure_condition: If the correlation between month-over-month INDPRO growth and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p>0.10) over rolling 2-year windows, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) growth below its trailing trend signals weakening consumer demand, a dominant driver of GDP, and anticipates downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 617719 million reflecting moderate consumer spending pace
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns following RSAFS deceleration triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) stays high, the combination signals persistent policy restraint with limited scope for near-term easing, compounding valuation pressure on equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days beyond either series individually.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains above 5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 273.012 with FEDFUNDS held at elevated cycle-peak levels in early 2007
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with sticky inflation
failure_condition: If the joint CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS trigger fails to produce forward 60-day returns significantly more negative than the FEDFUNDS-only signal (interaction term insignificant at p>0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In an expansion regime, continued positive real GDP growth (GDPC1) confirms sustained aggregate demand and corporate revenue momentum, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, but this relationship is expected to weaken or reverse as the expansion matures toward its later stages.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19360.6 billion chained dollars indicating continued but decelerating growth
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: If positive GDPC1 growth readings are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances during mid-cycle regimes, or the effect sign flips without a corresponding late-cycle regime shift, the hypothesis is rejected.
